# MenuFramework LLM Guide

This file is the canonical usage guide for AI assistants generating code with MenuFramework.
Follow the API below exactly. Do not invent classes, methods, annotations, YAML loaders, XML
formats, or plugin lifecycle abstractions that are not listed here.

## What MenuFramework Is

MenuFramework is a Java 21 library for Paper inventory menus. It is not a standalone Bukkit/Paper
plugin and it does not provide a `plugin.yml`. A consuming plugin must shade it into its own jar.

Primary package:

```java
com.github.hanielcota.menuframework
```

Use these entry points:

```java
MenuService service = MenuFramework.create(plugin);
MenuService service = MenuFramework.initialize(plugin);
MenuBuilder builder = MenuFramework.builder("menu-id", service);
MenuBuilder builder = MenuFramework.builder("menu-id"); // only after initialize(...)
```

Do not use:

```java
new MenuFramework(...)
MenuFramework.create(plugin).service()
MenuBuilder.builder()
builder.id(...)
builder.build(service)
```

Those APIs do not exist.

## Installation With JitPack

Use JitPack coordinates:

```groovy
repositories {
    mavenCentral()
    maven { url = 'https://repo.papermc.io/repository/maven-public/' }
    maven { url = 'https://jitpack.io' }
}

dependencies {
    compileOnly 'io.papermc.paper:paper-api:1.21.1-R0.1-SNAPSHOT'
    implementation 'com.github.HanielCota:MenuFramework:v1.0.0'
}
```

MenuFramework is published as a shadowed jar that already contains its runtime dependencies. In a
Paper plugin, still shade and relocate MenuFramework to avoid class conflicts between plugins:

```groovy
plugins {
    id 'java'
    id 'com.gradleup.shadow' version '9.3.2'
}

shadowJar {
    relocate 'com.github.hanielcota.menuframework', 'yourplugin.libs.menuframework'
    archiveClassifier.set('')
}

tasks.build.dependsOn tasks.shadowJar
```

## Plugin Lifecycle

Use one `MenuService` owned by your plugin. Shut it down in `onDisable`.

```java
public final class MyPlugin extends JavaPlugin {
    private MenuService menus;

    @Override
    public void onEnable() {
        menus = MenuFramework.create(this);
        registerMenus();
    }

    @Override
    public void onDisable() {
        if (menus != null) {
            menus.shutdown();
        }
    }
}
```

Use `MenuFramework.initialize(plugin)` only when you intentionally want singleton access through
`MenuFramework.service()` or `MenuFramework.builder("id")`.

## Basic Menu

Use `ItemTemplate` for menu items. Use `ctx` helpers for menu actions and messaging.

```java
private void registerMenus() {
    ItemTemplate filler = ItemTemplate.builder(Material.BLACK_STAINED_GLASS_PANE)
        .name(" ")
        .build();

    ItemTemplate shop = ItemTemplate.builder(Material.EMERALD)
        .name("<green>Shop")
        .lore(List.of(Component.text("Open the shop")))
        .glow(true)
        .build();

    MenuFramework.builder("main", menus)
        .rows(3)
        .title("<green>Main Menu")
        .fillEmpty(filler)
        .slot(13, shop, ctx -> {
            ctx.reply("<gray>Opening shop...");
            ctx.open("shop");
        })
        .build()
        .register();
}

public void openMain(Player player) {
    menus.open(player, "main").exceptionally(error -> {
        player.sendMessage(Component.text("Could not open menu."));
        return null;
    });
}
```

`MenuService.open(...)` returns `CompletableFuture<MenuSession>`.

## MenuBuilder API

Construct builders only through:

```java
MenuFramework.builder("id", menus)
MenuFramework.builder("id")
```

Supported builder methods:

```java
title(Component title)
title(String miniMessage)
rows(int rows)                         // 1..6 chest rows
layout(String... layout)               // each row uses up to 9 chars
bind(char c, ItemTemplate template)
bind(char c, ItemTemplate template, ClickHandler handler)
bindNavigational(char c, ItemTemplate template)
slot(int slot, ItemTemplate template, ClickHandler handler)
navigational(int slot, ItemTemplate template, ClickHandler handler)
slotWithCooldown(int slot, ItemTemplate template, ClickHandler handler, long cooldownTicks)
slotWithPermission(int slot, ItemTemplate template, ClickHandler handler, String permission, ItemTemplate fallback)
toggleSlot(int slot, ItemTemplate enabled, ItemTemplate disabled, boolean initialState, ToggleHandler handler)
fillBorder(ItemTemplate template)
fillEmpty(ItemTemplate template)
fillPattern(SlotPatternStrategy pattern, ItemTemplate template)
pagination(PaginationConfig pagination)
feature(MenuFeature feature)
allowPlayerInventoryClicks(boolean allow)
allowShiftClick(boolean allow)
onPlayerInventoryClick(PlayerInventoryClickHandler handler)
dynamicContent(DynamicContentProvider provider)
addItem(ItemTemplate template, ClickHandler handler)
build()
```

After `build()`, call `.register()`. A `MenuBuilder` is single-use; do not call `build()` twice.

## ItemTemplate API

Use `ItemTemplate.builder(Material)`:

```java
ItemTemplate item = ItemTemplate.builder(Material.DIAMOND)
    .name("<aqua>Diamond")
    .lore(List.of(Component.text("A menu item")))
    .amount(1)
    .glow(false)
    .customModelData(0)
    .build();
```

Supported builder methods:

```java
name(Component)
name(String miniMessage)
lore(List<Component>)
flags(ItemFlag...)
glow(boolean)
amount(int)              // 1..64
customModelData(int)
head(UUID)
head(String base64Texture)
leatherColor(Color)
clickSound(org.bukkit.Sound)
pdc(NamespacedKey, Object)
build()
```

Do not pass raw `ItemStack` into menu slots. Slots expect `ItemTemplate`.

## ClickContext API

Slot handlers receive `ClickContext ctx`.

Available methods:

```java
Player player()
Audience audience()
ClickType clickType()
MenuSession session()
int slot()
int rawSlot()
void open(String menuId)
void back()
boolean hasPreviousMenu()
void setPage(int page)
int currentPage()
void reply(Component message)
void reply(String miniMessage)
void close()
void refresh()
Plugin plugin()
```

Use `ctx.open("other-menu")` for menu navigation. Use `ctx.close()` to close. Use `ctx.refresh()`
to re-render the current menu.

## MenuSession API

`MenuSession` represents one open menu view:

```java
UUID viewerId()
String menuId()
InventoryView view()
int currentPage()
void setPage(int page)
void refresh()
void close()
boolean isSameView(InventoryView other)
CompletableFuture<Void> dispose()
void updateSlot(int slot, ItemTemplate template)
void updateSlots(Map<Integer, ItemTemplate> slots)
```

Prefer `updateSlot` or `updateSlots` for small visual changes. Use `refresh` when dynamic content
or pagination must be recalculated.

## Pagination

Use `PaginationConfig.builder()`.

```java
menus.registerTemplate("prev", ItemTemplate.builder(Material.ARROW).name("<yellow>Previous").build());
menus.registerTemplate("next", ItemTemplate.builder(Material.ARROW).name("<yellow>Next").build());

PaginationConfig pagination = PaginationConfig.builder()
    .contentSlots(List.of(10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16))
    .navigationSlots(List.of(45, 53))
    .previousTemplate("prev")
    .nextTemplate("next")
    .build();

MenuFramework.builder("shop", menus)
    .rows(6)
    .pagination(pagination)
    .dynamicContent((player, session) -> products.stream()
        .map(product -> SlotDefinition.of(-1, product.icon(), ctx -> buy(product, ctx.player())))
        .toList())
    .build()
    .register();
```

Dynamic content items for pagination normally use slot `-1`; the pagination engine projects them
into configured content slots.

Built-in `SlotPattern` values for pagination content:

```java
SlotPattern.FULL
SlotPattern.BORDERED
SlotPattern.CHEST_6
```

`PaginationConfig.Builder.contentSlots(SlotPattern pattern)` uses `pattern.slots()` with 6 rows.
For custom row counts, pass an explicit `List<Integer>`.

## Dynamic Content

Two valid approaches:

```java
builder.dynamicContent((player, session) -> List<SlotDefinition>);
menus.setDynamicContentProvider("menu-id", provider);
menus.setDynamicContent("menu-id", List<SlotDefinition>);
```

Use `DynamicContentProvider.of(list)` for fixed lists. Avoid blocking database or web calls inside
`provide(...)`; preload or cache external data before rendering when possible.

## Permissions, Cooldowns, Toggles

Permission slot:

```java
.slotWithPermission(22, adminItem, ctx -> openAdmin(ctx.player()), "myplugin.admin", deniedItem)
```

Cooldown slot:

```java
.slotWithCooldown(20, button, ctx -> runAction(ctx.player()), 20L)
```

Toggle slot:

```java
.toggleSlot(24, enabledItem, disabledItem, false, (ctx, enabled) -> {
    ctx.reply(enabled ? "<green>Enabled" : "<red>Disabled");
})
```

## Player Inventory Clicks

By default, player inventory clicks and shift-clicks are blocked while a menu is open.

```java
.allowPlayerInventoryClicks(true)
.allowShiftClick(false)
.onPlayerInventoryClick((player, clickType, slot, session) -> {
    player.sendMessage(Component.text("Clicked player inventory slot " + slot));
})
```

`onPlayerInventoryClick` only runs when player inventory clicks are allowed.

## Features

Built-in feature factories:

```java
MenuFeatures.soundOnOpen(net.kyori.adventure.sound.Sound sound)
MenuFeatures.soundOnClick(net.kyori.adventure.sound.Sound sound)
MenuFeatures.refreshInterval(long ticks)
```

Attach features with:

```java
.feature(MenuFeatures.soundOnOpen(sound))
.feature(MenuFeatures.refreshInterval(20L))
```

Custom features implement `MenuFeature` and override only needed callbacks:

```java
onOpen(MenuSession session)
onClose(MenuSession session)
onClick(ClickContext context)
onTick(MenuSession session, Player viewer)
```

## Preloading

Use the service preloader for expensive menus:

```java
menus.preloader().preload("shop");
menus.preloader().preload(player, "leaderboard");
menus.preloader().preloadAll("shop", "profile", "leaderboard");
menus.preloader().invalidate("shop");
menus.preloader().invalidateAll();
```

Preload methods return `CompletableFuture<Void>`.

## Diagnostics And Cleanup

Service methods:

```java
Optional<MenuDefinition> getDefinition(String id)
void unregisterDefinition(String menuId)
Optional<ItemTemplate> getTemplate(String id)
Optional<MenuSession> getSession(UUID playerUuid)
void closeSession(UUID playerUuid)
void closeAllSessions()
MenuMetrics getMetrics()
void shutdown()
```

Always call `shutdown()` from the owning plugin's `onDisable`.

## Common Mistakes To Avoid

Do not invent config-file loaders. This framework is code-first.
Do not create Bukkit inventories manually for MenuFramework menus.
Do not use raw `ItemStack` in `slot(...)`; use `ItemTemplate`.
Do not forget `.build().register()`.
Do not use `MenuFramework.builder("id")` unless singleton mode was initialized.
Do not assume menu opening is synchronous; handle the returned `CompletableFuture`.
Do not store a `MenuSession` forever; sessions are per open inventory and are disposed on close.
Do not create one `MenuService` per menu; create one service per plugin.
Do not skip shading and relocation in production plugins.
